extraction.one_line_summary: SCHACHMANN won from breakaway while YATES cracked for first time, losing 25-30 seconds to DUMOULIN, FROOME, and POZZOVIVO on final climb
tactical_patterns: SCHACHMANN repeatedly attacked PFINGSTEN in breakaway finish, wearing down mental state before final acceleration broke him.
DUMOULIN initiated GC attack on final climb that exposed YATES, forcing rivals FROOME and POZZOVIVO to follow.
YATES lost contact when podium rivals attacked together, suggesting collaborative anti-leader tactics emerged late race.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: first crisis of race, lost 25-30s on final climb after appearing unbreakable earlier
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: positive: at 24, showed tactical maturity by repeatedly attacking to break opponent mentally before final sprint
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: initiated crucial GC attack that cracked race leader, gaining time back with three days remaining
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: recovered from being dropped to counter-attack and gain time on pink jersey
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: positive: followed key attacks to gain time on race leader despite not initiating
rider_signals.PFINGSTEN Christoph: negative: repeatedly clawed back to breakaway but mental state destroyed by repeated accelerations before final sprint
